title: Do time-series prediction models even work?
author: Limp_Orchid2024
selftext:
I saw a youtube video where somebody made a time series based neural network accurately predicting the next candle. The creator claimed it to be working like Chat GPT, where the current already predicted words are used to predict the next word and so on until the entire text is generated.

Is this possible to do with candles and a neural network? LSTM based transformers are usually used to predict text, I feel like predicting the next candle is completely different. If I this is not the right way to make model, what is the right way.

P.S the model was working pretty accurately on test data and it didn't seem fake.

comments.author: Comfortable-Friend96
comments.body:
I did some research and i coded sentiment analysis dashboards.

If we talk about pure 'time-series" yes they can work, but you will have very big variations between years. Atleast, this is my own experience.

For exemple: i've notice that every friday, in crypto, there is a drop between 9am and 4pm, meaning you sell at 9PM and buy back at 4PM. 
The results were true for 2017, 2024 and 2025, that work very well, but not for the other years. This is the knowledge base ONLY on time series. 
So ... once that's done and you find something like that, you have to figure out, why does it work for those years and not the other years ? And this is the complicated part because this is hard to guess, and it can be multi factor equation. It can be news ... it can be rate cut ... it can be a lot of things.

Now to answer your question : YES it is possible, but i don't think anyone will share it with any of us

replies.author: Comfortable-Friend96
replies.body: Keep in mind, some hedge funds and big players are spending billions in R&D to do things like that, it is not impossible but too simple to be effective in my own opinion.
